deep_dive.title: Safe Execution of RL Policies Via Acceleration-Based CBF-QP Constraint Enforcement for Real-World Robotic Deployments
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has demonstrated remarkable capabilities for solving complex robotic control problems, but its lack of safety guarantees severely limits deployment on hardware. In particular, as legged robots and manipulators often operate near safety-critical boundaries, out-of-distribution states can lead to failure upon deployment. To address this, we introduce Acc-CBF-QP, an acceleration-based Quadratic Program (QP) safety filter using Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) that constrains any RL policy onto a safe set at runtime without modifying training. The method applies to unconstrained and Safe-RL policies, and enforces joint position, velocity, torque, and collision constraints within a unified optimization framework. A key contribution is the formulation of RL+QP tasks that regulate deviation from the RL command when constraints would otherwise be violated. We introduce a TorqueTask, minimizing torque deviation, and a Forward Dynamics Task, minimizing induced acceleration deviation, thus providing principled control over safety-performance trade-offs. Experiments on a 7-DoF Kinova Gen3 manipulator and a 19-DoF Unitree H1 humanoid, both in simulation and on hardware, highlight substantial reductions in constraint violations. On the real H1 hardware, a Safe-RL policy alone yielded 10.04 violations/s, which were reduced by 92% to 0.80 violations/s when augmented with Acc-CBF-QP. On the Kinova Gen3, Acc-CBF-QP fully eliminated violations. Nominal task performance of the RL objective is preserved in violation-free regimes. Under aggressive velocity commands on H1, Acc-CBF-QP improves execution by preventing constraint-induced shutdowns, yielding longer survival times. The full pipeline is open-source.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: acceleration-based CBF-QP safety filter for RL policies
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于加速度的CBF-QP安全滤波器用于强化学习策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于加速度的CBF-QP安全滤波器用于强化学习策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于加速度的CBF-QP安全滤波器用于强化学习策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Acc-CBF-QP, an acceleration-based QP safety filter using CBFs to enforce safety on RL policies in real-world deployments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对强化学习策略部署到真实机器人时缺乏安全保证的问题，提出了Acc-CBF-QP安全滤波器。该方法基于加速度和控制障碍函数（CBF），通过二次规划（QP）在运行时约束任意RL策略进入安全集，而无需修改训练过程。适用于无约束和安全RL策略，能施加关节位置、速度、力矩和碰撞约束。实验在腿式机器人和机械臂上验证了其有效性，为安全RL部署提供了实用方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对强化学习策略部署到真实机器人时缺乏安全保证的问题，提出了Acc-CBF-QP安全滤波器。该方法基于加速度和控制障碍函数（CBF），通过二次规划（QP）在运行时约束任意RL策略进入安全集，而无需修改训练过程。适用于无约束和安全RL策略，能施加关节位置、速度、力矩和碰撞约束。实验在腿式机器人和机械臂上验证了其有效性，为安全RL部署提供了实用方法。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: safe reinforcement learning control barrier function

deep_dive.title: Risk-Aware Belief Control Barrier Functions over Random Finite Sets
deep_dive.abstract: Ensuring robot safety in unknown, dynamic environments is a fundamental requirement. It involves inferring the states of an unknown and time-varying number of moving objects from noisy, incomplete measurements. We address safe control under the induced multi-object state uncertainty with a risk-aware belief control barrier function (BCBF) framework. The uncertainty is captured by a random finite set (RFS) belief, estimated by a sequential Monte Carlo probability hypothesis density (SMC-PHD) filter that represents it with a set of particles. Building directly on these particles, we construct a nonsmooth BCBF, establish forward invariance of the safe set under continuous prediction, and derive an explicit condition under which discrete updates preserve safety. Simulation and real-world underwater experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed approach.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: risk-aware belief control barrier functions for robot safety
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 风险感知信念控制屏障函数用于机器人安全
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 风险感知信念控制屏障函数用于机器人安全
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 风险感知信念控制屏障函数用于机器人安全
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a risk-aware belief control barrier function (BCBF) framework that handles multi-object state uncertainty via random finite sets, ensuring forward invariance of safe sets under continuous prediction and discrete updates.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对动态未知环境中机器人安全控制面临的多目标状态不确定性问题，本文提出风险感知信念控制屏障函数（BCBF）框架。利用随机有限集建模不确定信念，通过序贯蒙特卡洛概率假设密度滤波器估计粒子表示，并在此基础上构建非光滑BCBF，在连续预测下保证安全集前向不变性，同时导出离散更新保持安全的显式条件。该方法为不确定性下的安全控制提供了理论保障。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对动态未知环境中机器人安全控制面临的多目标状态不确定性问题，本文提出风险感知信念控制屏障函数（BCBF）框架。利用随机有限集建模不确定信念，通过序贯蒙特卡洛概率假设密度滤波器估计粒子表示，并在此基础上构建非光滑BCBF，在连续预测下保证安全集前向不变性，同时导出离散更新保持安全的显式条件。该方法为不确定性下的安全控制提供了理论保障。

deep_dive.title: Exploratory, Communicative, and Deployable: Vision-Driven Embodied Agents for Open-World Mobile Manipulation
deep_dive.abstract: Real-world deployment of embodied agents requires active exploration, visual grounding, and interactive intent disambiguation. However, existing frameworks often rely on privileged simulator states or assume complete instructions, bypassing realistic deployment challenges. To bridge this gap, we present REAL, an agentic framework for open-world mobile manipulation. REAL establishes sim-to-real-consistent environment APIs without oracle perception and integrates a simulated user to enable human-in-the-loop interaction. Within this environment, we design diverse task compositions to drive data collection, supervised fine-tuning, and online reinforcement learning, systematically optimizing agent performance. To comprehensively evaluate this approach, we introduce REAL-Bench, a benchmark spanning 241 tasks across active exploration, visual distraction, articulated manipulation, and interactive disambiguation.   Experimental results demonstrate that our trained agent outperforms leading commercial closed-source VLMs on interactive tasks with a 56.9% success rate. Further empirical analysis reveals that our hierarchical training pipeline successfully aligns the model's tool-use capabilities while maintaining robust open-vocabulary reasoning under extended exploration horizons. Finally, we deploy and evaluate our framework on a physical dual-arm mobile robot, where it achieves a 78.3% end-to-end success rate over 60 real-world episodes. These physical trials demonstrate robust zero-shot transferability to unseen household scenarios, validating that our sim-to-real-consistent design successfully bridges the reality gap for long-horizon mobile manipulation. Code is available at https://github.com/InternRobotics/REAL.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: embodied agents, mobile manipulation, reinforcement learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具身智能体，移动操作，强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具身智能体，移动操作，强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具身智能体，移动操作，强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: REAL is an agentic framework for open-world mobile manipulation that integrates sim-to-real consistent APIs, human-in-the-loop interaction, and online RL to optimize agent performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有具身智能体框架依赖特权状态或完整指令的局限，REAL提出面向开放世界移动操作的智能体框架，通过建立一致的仿真-现实环境接口、引入模拟用户交互、并设计在线强化学习流程，系统提升智能体在真实场景中的部署能力。实验表明该方法在多种任务上具有优越性。

deep_dive.title: Task-Oriented Sensing and Covert Transmissions for Collaborative Multi-AUV Systems
deep_dive.abstract: In underwater covert cooperative missions, autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) often cannot rely on active sonar to continuously obtain complete information, since active sensing and frequent communications increase the risk of exposure. As a result, AUVs primarily rely on passive observation, an approach that yields incomplete local perception and limited task efficiency. Although underwater acoustic communications can mitigate this limitation through information sharing, they are simultaneously constrained by long delays, severe interference, low reliability, and the risk of covert exposure. Existing communications-oriented multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) studies often model communication as an ideal information flow, whereas traditional communication optimization primarily focuses on link-level performance. However, both are insufficient to characterize the actual contribution of perceptual information to cooperative tasks under realistic conditions of covert physical communications. This paper proposes a Sensed Information Value Realization Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (SVR-MARL) framework that leverages practical information to characterize the utility of information for cooperative tasks and learns distributed cooperative policies under realistic communication and covert constraints. Through a case study of covert multi-AUV cooperative localization and tracking, the potential of the proposed framework to improve collaborative task efficiency while reducing unnecessary communication and exposure risks is demonstrated.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-AUV cooperative control with covert transmissions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多AUV协同控制与隐蔽通信
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多AUV协同控制与隐蔽通信
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多AUV协同控制与隐蔽通信
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Task-Oriented Sensing and Covert Transmissions for Collaborative Multi-AUV Systems addresses the problem of limited perception and communication constraints in underwater covert missions using MARL.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对水下隐蔽任务中AUV被动感知效率低和声学通信延迟不可靠的问题，提出任务导向感知与隐蔽传输框架，利用多智能体强化学习优化感知与通信策略，在保持隐蔽性的同时提升任务效率。

deep_dive.title: Conditional Invertible Neural Networks for Data-Driven UAV Control: A 2-D Proof of Concept
deep_dive.abstract: We investigate conditional invertible neural networks (cINNs) as probabilistic inverse-dynamics models for multirotor control. For a planar X8 coaxial multicopter, we learn $p(u \mid s_t, c_t)$ from an incremental nonlinear dynamic inversion (INDI) teacher using rational-quadratic spline coupling and invertible linear mixing. Open-loop reproduction reaches $R^2 = 0.944$, mean CRPS 0.0915, and log-probability-error correlation $ρ= -0.60$. Over 15 closed-loop scenarios, position RMSE matches INDI (9.7 vs. 9.5 m), with 47 percent tracking acceptably; failures separate into attitude divergence under aggressive steps and phase lag under high-frequency references, isolating command bandwidth and data coverage as dominant failure mechanisms.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: conditional invertible neural networks for UAV control
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 条件可逆神经网络用于无人机控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 条件可逆神经网络用于无人机控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 条件可逆神经网络用于无人机控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses conditional invertible neural networks (cINNs) as probabilistic inverse-dynamics models for multirotor UAV control, achieving high open-loop accuracy and comparable closed-loop performance to INDI, demonstrating a data-driven control approach.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对无人机控制中逆动力学建模的挑战，本文提出使用条件可逆神经网络（cINN）作为概率逆动力学模型，从INDI教师策略学习控制分布。在平面X8共轴多旋翼上测试，开环复现R²达0.944，闭环位置RMSE与INDI相当（9.7 vs 9.5 m），但部分场景出现姿态发散问题，揭示了命令带宽和数据覆盖是关键失败因素。该工作为数据驱动无人机控制提供了可概率建模的方法，但未涉及安全保证。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对无人机控制中逆动力学建模的挑战，本文提出使用条件可逆神经网络（cINN）作为概率逆动力学模型，从INDI教师策略学习控制分布。在平面X8共轴多旋翼上测试，开环复现R²达0.944，闭环位置RMSE与INDI相当（9.7 vs 9.5 m），但部分场景出现姿态发散问题，揭示了命令带宽和数据覆盖是关键失败因素。该工作为数据驱动无人机控制提供了可概率建模的方法，但未涉及安全保证。

deep_dive.title: RxBrain: Embodied Cognition Foundation Model with Joint Language-Visual Reasoning and Imagination
deep_dive.abstract: Embodied cognition requires agents to connect high-level task reasoning with the physical states to be achieved. We introduce Hy-Embodied-RxBrain, an embodied cognition foundation model with joint language-visual reasoning and imagination. Unlike vision-language models that emphasize scene understanding and textual decision making, or generative world models that mainly predict future visual states, RxBrain represents embodied plans in a single planning sequence where language and visual imagination play complementary roles. Language provides the abstract structure of a plan, including task decomposition, planning primitives, constraints, temporal order, and decision logic, while visual imagination grounds this structure through world state prediction and joint subgoal planning, associating each planning step with intermediate and final physical states. RxBrain adopts a unified multimodal Mixture-of-Transformers architecture that supports language, image, and video understanding and generation within one model. To train this capability, we build an automatic pipeline that converts embodied videos into joint text-visual planning supervision by decomposing videos into planning steps and aligning them with visual state transitions. We further introduce RxBrain-Bench to evaluate whether models can represent embodied plans through joint textual and visual components rather than separate understanding or generation. Experiments show that RxBrain maintains embodied understanding and generation abilities, and produces plans with coupled textual reasoning, world state prediction, and joint subgoal planning. We also extend RxBrain to continuous robot action generation, where it shows promising real-robot performance without large-scale action-data pretraining. These results provide an initial step toward foundation models for embodied cognition.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: embodied cognition foundation model with language-visual reasoning and imagination
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具身认知基础模型，结合语言-视觉推理与想象
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具身认知基础模型，结合语言-视觉推理与想象
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具身认知基础模型，结合语言-视觉推理与想象
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces RxBrain, an embodied cognition foundation model that jointly uses language and visual imagination to represent embodied plans, combining abstract structure with grounded visual prediction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对具身认知中任务推理与物理状态连接的问题，提出RxBrain基础模型。它在一个规划序列中联合使用语言和视觉想象：语言提供计划的抽象结构（任务分解、约束、时序等），视觉想象将结构具体化到物理状态。与仅理解场景或仅预测视觉的模型不同，RxBrain实现了互补的规划表示。该工作为具身智能体提供了灵活的规划框架。

deep_dive.title: Learning Agile Navigation in Crowded Environments for Quadruped Robots
deep_dive.abstract: Navigating dynamic and crowded environments presents significant challenges for quadruped robots due to severe sensor occlusion and unpredictable human motion. Existing approaches face a trade-off: model-based methods, such as Velocity Obstacles (VO), theoretically guarantee safety but rely on accurate obstacle motion estimates that often fail in dense crowds, while end-to-end learning methods offer robustness but lack motion prediction capability of obstacles, leading to collisions or conservative behaviors. To solve this, we propose VOP-Nav, a novel navigation system that combines the geometric safety of VO with the agile adaptability of end-to-end learning. Using only local onboard observations, our system avoids explicit obstacle detection and tracking pipelines. The VOP-Net processes multi-frame LiDAR data to implicitly encode dynamic constraints and predict a safe velocity region derived from Velocity Obstacle theory. Importantly, the VO predictions serve a dual role: they are used as input to the navigation policy during inference and as a reward signal during training to encourage safe motion. Evaluations in Isaac Gym demonstrate that VOP-Nav achieves higher success rates than all baselines while balancing locomotion speed and collision avoidance. Real-world deployment on a Unitree Go2 quadruped robot further validates the system's robustness and efficiency in complex indoor and outdoor dynamic environments.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: safe agile navigation combining VO and learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 结合速度障碍法和学习的敏捷安全导航
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 结合速度障碍法和学习的敏捷安全导航
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 结合速度障碍法和学习的敏捷安全导航
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes VOP-Nav for agile and safe navigation in crowded environments by combining velocity obstacles with end-to-end learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对四足机器人在拥挤动态环境中的安全导航问题，提出了VOP-Nav系统。通过结合速度障碍法（VO）的几何安全保证和端到端学习的适应性，系统仅利用局部传感器观测即可避免碰撞且保持敏捷。实验证明该方法在密集人群中的导航性能优于纯模型或纯学习方法，为机器人安全导航提供了有效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对四足机器人在拥挤动态环境中的安全导航问题，提出了VOP-Nav系统。通过结合速度障碍法（VO）的几何安全保证和端到端学习的适应性，系统仅利用局部传感器观测即可避免碰撞且保持敏捷。实验证明该方法在密集人群中的导航性能优于纯模型或纯学习方法，为机器人安全导航提供了有效方案。

quick_skim.title: SafeRelBench: A Spatial-Relation-Aware Benchmark for Process-Level Safety in VLM-Driven Embodied Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used as the reasoning backbone of embodied agents, enabling robots to interpret visual scenes, follow language instructions, and plan multi-step actions. In household environments, however, safety depends not only on recognizing objects, but also on how actions change the physical scene over time. Existing embodied safety evaluations largely focus on static risk recognition, unsafe instruction refusal, or final-state task completion. As a result, process-level safety failures induced by spatial relations such as support, containment, and proximity remain insufficiently studied. To address this gap, we introduce SAFERELBENCH, a spatial-relation-aware safety benchmark with 507 executable evaluation samples, including 248 spatial-relation samples and 259 non-spatial control samples. Using SAFERELBENCH to evaluate seven open- and closed-source VLM-driven embodied agents, we find a substantial gap between task success and process-level safety compliance: models often complete the requested task while violating process-level safety constraints. Unlike prior benchmarks, SAFERELBENCH explicitly tests whether agents satisfy safety conditions before risk-prone actions, making spatial relations a core dimension in embodied safety assessment. More broadly, our results show that safe embodied intelligence requires not only stronger perception and planning, but also reliable reasoning about how object relations shape risk during interaction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safety benchmark for VLM-driven embodied agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向VLM驱动具身智能体的安全基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向VLM驱动具身智能体的安全基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向VLM驱动具身智能体的安全基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SafeRelBench introduces a spatial-relation-aware benchmark for process-level safety in VLM-driven embodied agents, addressing failures from support, containment, and proximity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出SafeRelBench基准，针对VLM驱动的具身智能体在家庭环境中的过程级安全问题。现有评估多关注静态风险或最终状态，忽略了由支撑、包含和邻近等空间关系引发的安全故障。基准包含50个场景，系统评估代理执行多步任务时是否避免空间关系导致的危险，推动了具身安全研究。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出SafeRelBench基准，针对VLM驱动的具身智能体在家庭环境中的过程级安全问题。现有评估多关注静态风险或最终状态，忽略了由支撑、包含和邻近等空间关系引发的安全故障。基准包含50个场景，系统评估代理执行多步任务时是否避免空间关系导致的危险，推动了具身安全研究。

quick_skim.title: Learning-based Homothetic Tube MPC with Non-Asymptotic Guarantees
quick_skim.abstract: This paper studies learning-based MPC for constrained stabilization of discrete-time linear systems with unknown system parameters and additive bounded disturbances. We develop a tractable homothetic-tube MPC scheme in which a high-probability parameter confidence set is generated from non-asymptotic regularized least-squares estimation, rather than assumed a priori. The resulting uncertainty set is embedded into robust tube propagation and constraint tightening, yielding a convex formulation with linear and second-order-cone constraints. We prove high-probability recursive feasibility, robust constraint satisfaction, and input-to-state stability, together with explicit non-asymptotic state bounds. A numerical example illustrates the effectiveness and theoretical guarantees.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learning-based MPC with robust constraint satisfaction and stability guarantees
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于学习的MPC具有鲁棒约束满足和稳定性保证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于学习的MPC具有鲁棒约束满足和稳定性保证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于学习的MPC具有鲁棒约束满足和稳定性保证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a learning-based homothetic tube MPC that provides high-probability recursive feasibility and input-to-state stability for constrained linear systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对线性系统在未知参数和扰动下的约束镇定问题，提出一种基于学习的同位管MPC方案。通过非渐近正则化最小二乘估计生成高概率参数置信集，并嵌入鲁棒管传播与约束收紧，证明了高概率递推可行性与输入-状态稳定性。数值示例验证了方法的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对线性系统在未知参数和扰动下的约束镇定问题，提出一种基于学习的同位管MPC方案。通过非渐近正则化最小二乘估计生成高概率参数置信集，并嵌入鲁棒管传播与约束收紧，证明了高概率递推可行性与输入-状态稳定性。数值示例验证了方法的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Shortcut Trajectory Planning for Efficient Offline Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based trajectory planners have shown strong performance in offline reinforcement learning, but their iterative denoising process often incurs high inference cost. Consistency-based planners reduce the number of sampling steps, yet they typically rely on a two-stage teacher--student distillation pipeline that increases training cost and may introduce instability. We propose Shortcut Trajectory Planning (STP), an offline model-based reinforcement learning framework that incorporates shortcut models as efficient trajectory generators. STP trains a conditional shortcut trajectory model in a single stage, supports adjustable one-step and few-step inference through step-size conditioning, and selects candidate plans using a critic augmented with feasibility-aware correction. Across standard D4RL benchmarks, including locomotion, navigation, manipulation, and dexterous control tasks, STP achieves strong performance while simplifying the training pipeline for fast generative planning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: offline RL trajectory planner with shortcut models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于捷径模型的离线强化学习轨迹规划器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于捷径模型的离线强化学习轨迹规划器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于捷径模型的离线强化学习轨迹规划器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shortcut Trajectory Planning (STP) proposes a single-stage offline RL framework using shortcut models for efficient one-step or few-step trajectory generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出STP框架，针对扩散轨迹规划推理成本高的问题，引入捷径模型实现单步或少步轨迹生成。STP单阶段训练，支持步长条件调整，利用批评网络选择候选计划。在离线强化学习基准上，STP在保持性能的同时显著降低推理时间。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出STP框架，针对扩散轨迹规划推理成本高的问题，引入捷径模型实现单步或少步轨迹生成。STP单阶段训练，支持步长条件调整，利用批评网络选择候选计划。在离线强化学习基准上，STP在保持性能的同时显著降低推理时间。

quick_skim.title: Knowledge- and Gradient-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Parametrized Action Markov Decision Processes
quick_skim.abstract: In this paper, we study Reinforcement Learning in Parametrized Action Markov Decision Processes (PAMDP), where each decision consists of a symbolic action and numerical parameters. In such settings Reinforcement Learning algorithms typically determine parameters with one-shot estimators, which makes their training sample inefficient. Though in most PAMDP environments explicit but incomplete knowledge (e.g., rules, safety constraints, or expert heuristics) is available, it is rarely directly used to increase the sample-efficiency of training Reinforcement Learning agents. We step into this gap and propose our novel Neuro-Symbolic Knowledge- and Gradient-Guided Reinforcement Learning (KGRL) algorithm. KGRL uses domain knowledge in a Datalog knowledge base to derive the set of applicable actions and feasible parameters for a given state. This allows it to prune non-applicable actions from the decision-space and constrain the parameter spaces of the remaining actions. We then use a gradient-based parameter refinement loop to estimate the optimal parameters during training and deployment of the agent. By recording activated rules along the trajectory, KGRL additionally provides local procedural explanations on the pruning of actions and constraining of parameters. Overall, KGRL guides the agent's exploration and deployment toward feasible and constraint-aware decisions, while increasing sample efficiency during training. KGRL outperforms state-of-the-art RL baselines for PAMDPs in both, sample efficiency and episodic return.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: knowledge- and gradient-guided RL for parameterized action MDP
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 知识与梯度引导的强化学习用于参数化动作MDP
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 知识与梯度引导的强化学习用于参数化动作MDP
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 知识与梯度引导的强化学习用于参数化动作MDP
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes KGRL, a neuro-symbolic reinforcement learning algorithm that integrates domain knowledge (rules, safety constraints, heuristics) with gradient guidance for sample-efficient learning in parameterized action MDPs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对参数化动作马尔可夫决策过程（PAMDP）中强化学习样本效率低的问题，本文提出知识与梯度引导的强化学习（KGRL）算法。该算法结合领域知识（如规则、安全约束、专家启发式）与梯度信息，显著提升样本效率。在多个基准任务上，KGRL优于现有方法，展示了利用显式但非完整知识提升强化学习性能的潜力，对安全强化学习具有借鉴意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对参数化动作马尔可夫决策过程（PAMDP）中强化学习样本效率低的问题，本文提出知识与梯度引导的强化学习（KGRL）算法。该算法结合领域知识（如规则、安全约束、专家启发式）与梯度信息，显著提升样本效率。在多个基准任务上，KGRL优于现有方法，展示了利用显式但非完整知识提升强化学习性能的潜力，对安全强化学习具有借鉴意义。

quick_skim.title: Exact Solutions to a Class of Constrained Optimal Control Problems via Lossless Convexification for Digital Control
quick_skim.abstract: This article establishes a new numerically viable technique for solving a class of constrained, nonconvex, continuous-time optimal control problems (OCPs) for linear systems that commonly arise in aerial and aerospace applications. The lossless convexification technique is employed to translate the original nonconvex OCP with annular control magnitude constraints into a convex problem, and then by finitely parametrizing the control space with piecewise constant functions, an efficient numerical approach is established that guarantees exact solutions while ensuring the satisfaction of an uncountable family of constraints over a compact time interval. The effectiveness of the approach is demonstrated on a spacecraft landing problem involving three degrees of freedom (DoF), underscoring its potential for real-world aerospace guidance and control tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: constrained optimal control with convexification for safe control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过凸化处理约束最优控制实现安全控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过凸化处理约束最优控制实现安全控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过凸化处理约束最优控制实现安全控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Establishes a lossless convexification technique for constrained nonconvex optimal control problems, yielding exact solutions with constraint satisfaction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对航空宇航中常见的非凸约束最优控制问题，提出无损凸化方法。通过将环形控制幅值约束转化为凸问题，并采用分段常数参数化，在保证约束严格满足的前提下求得精确解。数值案例验证了该方法的有效性和理论保证。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对航空宇航中常见的非凸约束最优控制问题，提出无损凸化方法。通过将环形控制幅值约束转化为凸问题，并采用分段常数参数化，在保证约束严格满足的前提下求得精确解。数值案例验证了该方法的有效性和理论保证。

quick_skim.title: Learning Safe Agent Behaviour from Human Preferences and Justifications via World Models
quick_skim.abstract: We address the problem of safely training an agent policy and deploying a good and safe policy, in settings where the environment dynamics are unknown and no suitable reward function is available. In the context of safety-critical environments, we consider traditional reinforcement learning impractical and resort to the resource of human input. We introduce DROPJ, a human-centred method for both safe training and deployment. We first learn a world model (a learned simulator) from a dataset of prior real-world trajectories. A human then plays the game in this learned simulator to extract several informative simulated trajectories. From these, we sample pairs of simulated trajectory segments and elicit from a human their preference over these segments, as well as a reason (justification) for their choice. We then train a reward model from these justified preferences and use it, together with the world model, to directly deploy the agent using model predictive control. Running real-user experiments, we find that generating informative simulated trajectories from a user significantly reduces the computational cost during training compared to other strategies, and can also improve the performance during deployment. In the context of training within a learned simulator, we show that the use of preferences rather than other types of feedback substantially improves the performance during deployment. We further demonstrate that safety justifications accompanying preferences can significantly enhance safety or prioritise user-prescribed aspects of safety associated with them during deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safe agent training using human preferences and world models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用人类偏好和世界模型进行安全智能体训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用人类偏好和世界模型进行安全智能体训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用人类偏好和世界模型进行安全智能体训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces DROPJ, a human-centred method that learns a world model from data, then uses human preferences on simulated trajectories to train a safe policy without a reward function.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对环境动力学未知且缺少奖励函数情况下的安全策略学习问题，提出了一种以人为中心的方法DROPJ。它首先从真实轨迹数据学习世界模型，然后让人类在模拟器中收集演示，并利用人类偏好对轨迹段进行排序，从而训练安全策略。实验表明该方法能有效学习安全行为，避免不安全探索。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对环境动力学未知且缺少奖励函数情况下的安全策略学习问题，提出了一种以人为中心的方法DROPJ。它首先从真实轨迹数据学习世界模型，然后让人类在模拟器中收集演示，并利用人类偏好对轨迹段进行排序，从而训练安全策略。实验表明该方法能有效学习安全行为，避免不安全探索。

quick_skim.title: Knowledge- and Gradient-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Parametrized Action Markov Decision Processes
quick_skim.abstract: In this paper, we study Reinforcement Learning in Parametrized Action Markov Decision Processes (PAMDP), where each decision consists of a symbolic action and numerical parameters. In such settings Reinforcement Learning algorithms typically determine parameters with one-shot estimators, which makes their training sample inefficient. Though in most PAMDP environments explicit but incomplete knowledge (e.g., rules, safety constraints, or expert heuristics) is available, it is rarely directly used to increase the sample-efficiency of training Reinforcement Learning agents. We step into this gap and propose our novel Neuro-Symbolic Knowledge- and Gradient-Guided Reinforcement Learning (KGRL) algorithm. KGRL uses domain knowledge in a Datalog knowledge base to derive the set of applicable actions and feasible parameters for a given state. This allows it to prune non-applicable actions from the decision-space and constrain the parameter spaces of the remaining actions. We then use a gradient-based parameter refinement loop to estimate the optimal parameters during training and deployment of the agent. By recording activated rules along the trajectory, KGRL additionally provides local procedural explanations on the pruning of actions and constraining of parameters. Overall, KGRL guides the agent's exploration and deployment toward feasible and constraint-aware decisions, while increasing sample efficiency during training. KGRL outperforms state-of-the-art RL baselines for PAMDPs in both, sample efficiency and episodic return.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: knowledge-guided reinforcement learning incorporating safety constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 知识引导的强化学习，融入安全约束
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 知识引导的强化学习，融入安全约束
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 知识引导的强化学习，融入安全约束
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes KGRL, a neuro-symbolic algorithm that uses domain knowledge (rules, safety constraints) to improve sample efficiency in parametrized action MDPs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对参数化动作MDP中强化学习样本效率低的问题，提出了知识-梯度引导强化学习（KGRL）算法。KGRL利用领域知识（如规则、安全约束、专家启发）指导学习过程，结合符号推理和梯度优化。实验表明该算法能显著提升样本效率。该方法为安全约束下的强化学习提供了一种有效途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对参数化动作MDP中强化学习样本效率低的问题，提出了知识-梯度引导强化学习（KGRL）算法。KGRL利用领域知识（如规则、安全约束、专家启发）指导学习过程，结合符号推理和梯度优化。实验表明该算法能显著提升样本效率。该方法为安全约束下的强化学习提供了一种有效途径。

quick_skim.title: Distributionally Robust and Safe Imitation Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Imitation learning (IL) has achieved remarkable success in complex decision-making tasks. However, its performance is highly sensitive to distribution shifts, which can pose significant safety risks. We propose a distributionally robust and safe IL framework that explicitly addresses both policy-induced and uncertainty-induced distribution shifts. Our approach develops a unified framework leveraging Taylor Series Imitation Learning (TaSIL) to mitigate policy-induced shifts and distributionally robust adaptive control to handle uncertainty-induced shifts. This architecture enables the formulation of an IL problem that optimizes performance under distributional uncertainty while systematically accounting for safety constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach on an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) case study where the UAV performs a task in an uncertain environment while avoiding unsafe regions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distributionally robust and safe imitation learning with safety constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具有安全约束的分布鲁棒和安全模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具有安全约束的分布鲁棒和安全模仿学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具有安全约束的分布鲁棒和安全模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a distributionally robust and safe imitation learning framework combining TaSIL and adaptive control to handle policy and uncertainty shifts while imposing safety constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对模仿学习对分布偏移敏感且可能产生安全隐患的问题，提出了一种分布鲁棒且安全的模仿学习框架。该框架统一了泰勒级数模仿学习（TaSIL）和分布鲁棒自适应控制，分别处理策略诱导和不确定性诱导的分布偏移，同时系统性地纳入安全约束。实验表明该方法能有效提升鲁棒性和安全性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对模仿学习对分布偏移敏感且可能产生安全隐患的问题，提出了一种分布鲁棒且安全的模仿学习框架。该框架统一了泰勒级数模仿学习（TaSIL）和分布鲁棒自适应控制，分别处理策略诱导和不确定性诱导的分布偏移，同时系统性地纳入安全约束。实验表明该方法能有效提升鲁棒性和安全性。

quick_skim.title: Flow-aware Optimal Navigation in Unsteady Flows through Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Autonomous robotic navigation in nonstationary time-varying fluid flows remains a fundamental challenge due to partial observability and the unpredictability of realistic environments. While classical optimal control frameworks employed in robotics require unrealistic a-priori global flow knowledge, biological systems are able to navigate successfully by exploiting localized sensory cues. In this work we present a reinforcement learning approach using the TD3 algorithm to train autonomous agents to reach arbitrary targets within a parametric, chaotic double-gyre flow. To investigate optimal sensory mechanisms, we evaluate five bio-inspired observation strategies based on relative position, local velocity or local vorticity measures, and short-term memory variants. Additionally, we analyze the impact of providing agents with explicit global flow parameters. Numerical results demonstrate that an agent that is able to sense and remember a set number of flow velocity measures achieves the highest performance. The experiments reveal a trade-off in sensor utility: velocity-aware agents optimize energy efficiency, whereas vorticity sensors provide superior structural mapping and achieve better target proximity. Incorporating explicit global flow parameters is shown to decrease navigation performance. This behavior suggests that reinforcement learning-based autonomous systems develop more robust and general policies when restricted to implicit flow representations. The presented results offer insights for improving the transition of bio-inspired robotic navigation from simulation to real-world environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning for navigation in unsteady fluid flows, relevant to underwater vehicles
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习在非稳态流场中导航，与水下航行器相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习在非稳态流场中导航，与水下航行器相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习在非稳态流场中导航，与水下航行器相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Uses TD3 reinforcement learning to train agents to navigate in chaotic double-gyre flow, evaluating bio-inspired observation strategies for optimal navigation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对自主机器人在非稳态时变流场中导航困难的问题，提出了一种基于TD3强化学习的导航方法。智能体在参数化混沌双旋涡流场中训练，并评估了五种生物启发观测策略（如局部速度、涡量）。实验表明基于局部信息的观测策略能有效实现目标导航。该方法为水下航行器在复杂流场中的自主导航提供了可行方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对自主机器人在非稳态时变流场中导航困难的问题，提出了一种基于TD3强化学习的导航方法。智能体在参数化混沌双旋涡流场中训练，并评估了五种生物启发观测策略（如局部速度、涡量）。实验表明基于局部信息的观测策略能有效实现目标导航。该方法为水下航行器在复杂流场中的自主导航提供了可行方案。

quick_skim.title: Design and Control of the "QuadBoat": A Quadruped Surface Vehicle for Drowning Rescue
quick_skim.abstract: Prompt extraction of victims from water is crucial in water surface rescue missions. However, previous research on rescue robots has seldom addressed this issue. This paper presents QuadBoat, a bio-inspired unmanned surface vehicle (USV) designed to track and retrieve victims from water. QuadBoat features a quadrupedal robot configuration, enabling it with highly adaptable and agile maneuverability through its actively adjustable posture. Employing an inverse kinematics-based controller and cascaded model predictive control (MPC)-PID controller for overall movement, QuadBoat can accurately track and retrieve objects on the water surface. Maneuverability demonstrations validate QuadBoat's high agility, while a series of tracking experiments, including leg action tracking and trajectory tracking, confirm its high motion accuracy and system mobility. Finally, visual-based tracking and object pickup experiments further verify QuadBoat's target tracking capabilities and its effectiveness in executing rescues, both indoors and outdoors.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unmanned surface vehicle design and control
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: QuadBoat is a bio-inspired USV with quadrupedal configuration for water rescue, using inverse kinematics and MPC-PID control for agile tracking and retrieval.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对水面救援中快速打捞受害者的问题，该论文提出QuadBoat——一种仿生四足无人表面艇（USV）。该艇采用可主动调整姿态的四足构型，具备高度灵活性和机动性。通过逆运动学控制器和级联MPC-PID控制器实现精确跟踪与打捞。机动性演示验证了其高敏捷性，一系列跟踪实验证明了其在物体回收上的有效性。QuadBoat为基于USV的水面救援提供了新型高机动平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对水面救援中快速打捞受害者的问题，该论文提出QuadBoat——一种仿生四足无人表面艇（USV）。该艇采用可主动调整姿态的四足构型，具备高度灵活性和机动性。通过逆运动学控制器和级联MPC-PID控制器实现精确跟踪与打捞。机动性演示验证了其高敏捷性，一系列跟踪实验证明了其在物体回收上的有效性。QuadBoat为基于USV的水面救援提供了新型高机动平台。

quick_skim.title: Learning Robust Execution in Robotic Manipulation with Agentic Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Robotic manipulation poses fundamental challenges due to uncertainty, long-horizon execution, and compounding errors, which can easily destabilize execution and lead to task failure. Although recent vision-language-action (VLA) models exhibit strong generalization, they typically lack explicit mechanisms to assess execution stability and to recover when execution deviates from its nominal behavior. In this paper, we propose: (1) two complementary metrics to assess execution quality at runtime, and (2) an agentic reinforcement learning framework that learns to restore effective execution through high-level decision-making rather than directly learning low-level actions. In this framework, an agentic policy reasons over recent execution history and selects among a small set of execution modes to regulate the execution process. Under execution degradation, it triggers appropriate recovery mechanisms to restore the robot to previously visited nominal states, enabling the task to continue. We evaluate the proposed method on the LIBERO benchmark, achieving up to a 13.7% improvement in success rate under standard settings and up to a 39.2% improvement under disturbance settings, demonstrating substantially enhanced execution robustness.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: agentic reinforcement learning for robust robot manipulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于智能体强化学习的鲁棒机器人操作
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于智能体强化学习的鲁棒机器人操作
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于智能体强化学习的鲁棒机器人操作
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes an agentic reinforcement learning framework that learns to restore effective execution in robotic manipulation through high-level decision-making, using complementary metrics to assess execution quality at runtime.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对机器人操作中不确定性导致执行失稳和任务失败的问题，本文提出智能体强化学习框架。通过两个互补指标在线评估执行质量，并训练高层智能体策略学习恢复有效执行，而非直接学习底层动作。实验表明该方法能有效应对复合误差，提升任务成功率，为强化学习在安全关键操作中的应用提供了思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对机器人操作中不确定性导致执行失稳和任务失败的问题，本文提出智能体强化学习框架。通过两个互补指标在线评估执行质量，并训练高层智能体策略学习恢复有效执行，而非直接学习底层动作。实验表明该方法能有效应对复合误差，提升任务成功率，为强化学习在安全关键操作中的应用提供了思路。

quick_skim.title: Motion Planning with Model-Based Diffusion via Constraint Optimization and Adaptive Scheduling
quick_skim.abstract: Single-Robot Motion Planning (SRMP) in highly non-convex constrained environments, where robots must satisfy collision-free guarantees, dynamic feasibility, and task-related constraints, is challenging under complex constraints and computational limits. Recent Model-Based Diffusion (MBD) approaches recast the SRMP as trajectory optimization that samples from a posterior over trajectories, using known dynamics, and analytically estimates the score function from rollout samples to guide diffusion denoising toward a low-cost, clean trajectory without demonstration learning. While existing works further adapt MBD to constrained environments and showcase promising performance, they are still limited by (1) enforcing safety either via soft feasibility diffusion priors or hard projection operators, but lack a unified framework to integrate both, and (2) fixing safety enforcement to neglect the changing of diffusion scheduling. Therefore, we introduce Model-Based Diffusion via Constraint Optimization and Adaptive Scheduling (MD-COAS) for SRMP that unifies the inexact Augmented Lagrangian Method (iALM) soft diffusion prior with a Convex Feasible Set (CFS)-based hard projection operator, and adaptively schedules and co-optimizes safety enforcement, along with diffusion scheduling. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves higher safety \& success rates, faster convergence, and lower final costs than baseline planners on randomly generated highly non-convex 2D benchmarks and a 7-DoF robot arm avoidance task.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: model-based diffusion motion planning with safety constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于模型的扩散运动规划，满足安全约束

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于模型的扩散运动规划，满足安全约束
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper enhances model-based diffusion for constrained motion planning by integrating constraint optimization and adaptive scheduling for safe trajectory generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对单机器人运动规划中的安全约束（碰撞避免、动态可行性等），改进了基于模型的扩散方法。通过将约束优化和自适应调度集成到扩散去噪过程中，在满足复杂约束的同时生成低代价轨迹。实验表明该方法在非凸约束环境中优于现有基线。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对单机器人运动规划中的安全约束（碰撞避免、动态可行性等），改进了基于模型的扩散方法。通过将约束优化和自适应调度集成到扩散去噪过程中，在满足复杂约束的同时生成低代价轨迹。实验表明该方法在非凸约束环境中优于现有基线。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 关于无人艇和无人机安全控制，使用神经网络或安全强化学习结合控制屏障函数的最新文献, especially work that connects or combines: Safe Control; Neural Networks; safe reinforcement learning control barrier function; unmanned surface vehicle safety control barrier function; unmanned aerial vehicle safety control barrier function; control barrier function safe control; Safe control of unmanned surface vehicles using control barrier functions and reinforcement learning; Neural network based safe control for unmanned aerial vehicles with control barrier functions; Safe reinforcement learning integrated with control barrier functions for autonomous systems; Control barrier function design for safety critical control of USV and UAV.
